description:
OL 316 (REV. 7/2020) WWW
Autonomous Mode 
Conventional Mode
Additional information attached.
On January 29, 2026 at 9:00 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (“Waymo AV”) operating in Los Angeles, California was in a collision 
involving an SUV on W. Pico Boulevard near S. Sepulveda Boulevard. 
 
The Waymo AV was stopped facing northeast on W. Pico Boulevard in the second from the right lane in a queue of traffic for a red light at 
the intersection with S. Sepulveda Boulevard when a pedestrian approached the stationary Waymo AV and made contact with the front right 
sensor, causing the Waymo AV to remain stopped in lane. While the Waymo AV was stopped, an SUV that was stopped directly behind the 
Waymo AV proceeded to change lanes to the left lane and the right side of the SUV made contact with the rear left side of the stationary 
Waymo AV.  At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV’s Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained 
damage. 
 
Print
Clear Form
